title: Rosa pimpinellifolia subalpina
intro: Rosa pimpinellifolia subalpina, also known as alpine rose or Scottish rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa pimpinellifolia species, a type of wild rose native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Rosa pimpinellifolia subalpina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall health and well-being, as well as to treat specific conditions such as digestive issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Rosa pimpinellifolia subalpina is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a type of wild rose that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe.
